channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: US-Israel strikes continue as Iran war enters sixth day
item.description: Middle East war enters 6th day with Israel and the U.S. continuing to hit Iran as the conflict continues to spread across the region.
item.content:encoded: Middle East war enters 6th day with Israel and the U.S. continuing to hit Iran as the conflict continues to spread across the region.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: House to vote after Senate failed to constrain Trump's war in Iran
item.description: The Senate did not approve a measure to constrain President Trump's actions in Iran the House is set to vote on a similar measure on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: The Senate did not approve a measure to constrain President Trump's actions in Iran the House is set to vote on a similar measure on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: Sen. Ron Johnson opposes Congress voting on Iran war because it would 'show us divided'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Republican Sen. Ron Johnson of Wisconsin for his perspective of the conflict in the Middle East and the failed Senate effort to restrain U.S. military actions against Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Republican Sen. Ron Johnson of Wisconsin for his perspective of the conflict in the Middle East and the failed Senate effort to restrain U.S. military actions against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Middle East conflict enters Day 6, clerics convene to replace Iran's supreme leader, China to send special envoy to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war.
item.content:encoded: Middle East conflict enters Day 6, clerics convene to replace Iran's supreme leader, China to send special envoy to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Nominees talk about new Oscars category: casting
item.description: The Academy Awards will for the first time spotlight a vital, often overlooked craft: casting. NPR speaks with all the nominees in the new category.
item.content:encoded: The Academy Awards will for the first time spotlight a vital, often overlooked craft: casting. NPR speaks with all the nominees in the new category.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Massachusetts cinema collects mispronounced film titles
item.description: The employees of a movie theater have been keeping a list of some of the mis-remembered film titles that ticket-buyers have asked for.
item.content:encoded: The employees of a movie theater have been keeping a list of some of the mis-remembered film titles that ticket-buyers have asked for.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Concerns about maintenance of aircraft carrier USS Ford
item.description: The aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford has been at the center of two major conflicts in recent months. Some parents of crew members and lawmakers worry about the impact of its lengthy deployment.
item.content:encoded: The aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford has been at the center of two major conflicts in recent months. Some parents of crew members and lawmakers worry about the impact of its lengthy deployment.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Counterterrorism expert discusses possible domestic risks after US strikes on Iran
item.description: What are the domestic risks of terror attacks following U.S. strikes on Iran? NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Matthew Levitt, a counterterrorism expert at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy.
item.content:encoded: What are the domestic risks of terror attacks following U.S. strikes on Iran? NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Matthew Levitt, a counterterrorism expert at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Pan dulce is a sweet morning treat and a symbol of Latino culture
item.description: Pan dulce is a symbol of Latino food and culture. At a recent masterclass, people learned how to bake sweet bread while sharing stories.
item.content:encoded: Pan dulce is a symbol of Latino food and culture. At a recent masterclass, people learned how to bake sweet bread while sharing stories.
item.dc:creator: Marian Navarro

item.title: Dissident Mehdi Mahmoudian on his hopes for Iran now
item.description: Mehdi Mahmoudian has been in and out of Iran's prisons over decades. He's a longtime political and human rights activist. NPR's Leila Fadel spoke with him about what its like in Tehran.
item.content:encoded: Mehdi Mahmoudian has been in and out of Iran's prisons over decades. He's a longtime political and human rights activist. NPR's Leila Fadel spoke with him about what its like in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
